npc-notes/royal-leadership/hans-stera.ht.title: Hans Stera
npc-notes/royal-leadership/hans-stera.ht.description: Recently ascended to the throne of Steralis after the untimely death of his father, Sten Stera. As he is still a young child, a royal council issues decrees in his stead until he comes of age.

npc-notes/royal-leadership/henry-decker..title: Henry Decker
npc-notes/royal-leadership/henry-decker..description: Recently commissioned as Grand Marshall of the combined Royal Forces of Steralis

npc-notes/royal-leadership/sten-stera.ht.title: Sten Stera
npc-notes/royal-leadership/sten-stera.ht.description: The late king of Steralis, recently passed away unexpectedly at the age of 28.

npc-notes/tal-astero/artria.html.title: Artria
npc-notes/tal-astero/artria.html.description: A Lancer. Very sturdy, 6'2. Ethan: "It would be perfectly understandable if you mistook her for a mech." Lives on Steralis, is from and stationed at Tal Astero. She, Gwen, and Lance Beal are childhood friends.

npc-notes/tal-astero/governer-artem-ples.title: Governer Artem Pless
npc-notes/tal-astero/governer-artem-ples.description: Governor of Tal Astero.

npc-notes/tal-astero/gwen.html.title: Gwen
npc-notes/tal-astero/gwen.html.description: A lancer; 5'8. Lives on Steralis, is from and stationed at Tal Astero. She, Artria, and Lance Beal are childhood friends.

npc-notes/tal-astero/lance-beal.html.title: Lance Beal
npc-notes/tal-astero/lance-beal.html.description: A newly promoted officer. He's about 5'2, neatly cropped black hair, and dressed with a formal military uniform. From Steralis, stationed at Tal Astero (which is his hometown), and is on his first command. He, Gwen, and Artria are childhood friends.(couldn't make out what type of mech he has)

npc-notes/walisan/regional-commander-son.title: Regional Commander Sonja Creen
npc-notes/walisan/regional-commander-son.description: Mid 40s, wears a very red uniform. Regional commander on Steralis. Is in charge of training up the local troops for a grand unified army. She's apparently a formidable lancer.

pc's/backstory/cooper-ellys-(backstory)..title: Cooper Ellys (Backstory)
pc's/backstory/cooper-ellys-(backstory)..description:
To talk about Cooper Ellys, we should probably start by talking about her father. Chrom Ellys was a lancer himself, the product of some genetic engineering from some shadowy group that was happy to stay out of his life afterward– except for the odd undisclosed favor here and there, of course. Chrom filled the rest of his time with… well, we’ll get to that. For now, we’ll say he did pretty well for himself, married Cooper’s mom, and set up a residence in the city, managing a consortium of mines that he semi-illicitly owned. 
The division of responsibility in the family was that Cooper, her younger sister, and her mom would cover the home front, and her dad would go off to do lancer things and handle “business”, only infrequently coming home. It’s probably fair to think of him as having a rather 1920’s view of fatherhood: a good provider, but authoritarian, distant, and sometimes domineering or callous. Cooper didn’t enjoy the dutiful daughter, but the times she had to do it were infrequent enough, and she had a happy enough childhood mostly staying out of his way. She spent her time exploring the city and slumming with her friends, spending most of her considerable allowance paying various bribes to keep them (and occasionally herself) out of trouble. It would be years before Cooper or her mother understood the full extent of what her father was really doing.What he was doing, basically, was becoming one of the de facto kings of the planet’s underworld.
The planet, which I’ve been calling Eschaton but could be relocated/reflavored, is fairly Earthlike and about a third of the size. It’s what would happen if the Gilded Age took hold somewhere and never really stopped: think 1930’s tech with mass industrialization aided by investment trickling in from Union, with an elite class of oil barons and other captains of industry, whose activities bleed slowly from business ventures into sprawling Chicago mob-style criminal enterprises. Wealth inequality is staggering and graft is even worse, with most of the post-scarcity largesse siphoned off by corporate barons to reinforce their positions. Government is nominally a republic, but it’s so choked that it’s functionally a slightly-rotating oligarchy. It’s quite peripheral, such that having even five mechs on the surface at one time would constitute an extremely busy day. Chrom, with a mech there full-time, found himself uniquely positioned to shop his services around to all sorts of wealthy people in need of some hard power that was both virtually unstoppable and plausibly deniable. The other powers-that-be essentially guaranteed his monopoly: if anyone else had tried to organize the independent ventures that he did, one of the great corporations would swallow them up. But Chrom’s mech was a counterthreat no one with pre-WWII weapons was prepared to meet, at least not for very long. Soon, Chrom was working with groups of people who would never have worked directly with each other, weaving grey- and black- market ventures that spanned a continent.This went on for years, until Cooper was about nineteen, when her father died under mysterious circumstances– he was found unmoving and apparently uninjured in his mech, already cold. It quickly became clear that his death was quite a bit more mysterious than it originally seemed: apparently, Chrom had availed himself of illicit cloning technologies, probably on the say-so of whoever originally sponsored his modifications. There had been more than ten Chroms running around, each one carrying the threat of mech deployment wherever he went. Most of them had families. All of them were involved in different ventures to different degrees, and involved with each other to varying degrees. And, on that March day, all of them dropped dead without warning. The infidelities and new relations were a nasty shock, but nothing compared to the planet-wide fiasco of untangling Chrom’s estate. There was money attached to tons of things, many of which were tied to secrets that could topple half the ruling class (and therefore eagerly pursued by the rest). Much of it was poorly documented or intentionally obscured. It wasn’t even clear if it was correct to treat Chrom as one person. Without much backing, Cooper and her mom walked away with very little, but for one bit of fortune– Cooper was the only child of any Chrom to show signs of Chrom’s own genetic enhancements. So, in exchange for a firm they were likely to lose anyway, what they got were Chrom’s licenses. Since then, Cooper has been trying to unbury herself from the unsavory aspects of her dad’s past. Underneath all the estate divisions, there lurked a murkier world of deals her father had never committed to electronics, kept only in a hand-written notebook she recovered from a compartment in his mech. It was full of favors owed and credited; both were mostly to the kind of people Cooper would have been happy never to meet. It would be fair to call Cooper chaotic good, I think, but her chief motivation is getting clear of that stuff and starting her own life, as a lancer and otherwise: she’s tired of existing in a superposition of “fabulously wealthy” and “probably dead”, and worried that her family might get caught in the crosshairs if she gets complacent. The way she sees it, the more of her family’s old antagonists she can help or charm into being friends, the better off everyone will be– we’re all at least starting from the common ground of being kind of pissed at her dad. Anything that will help Cooper cross something off that list will get her where you need her to be, even if it’s off-planet. A quick word about genetic engineering: I very intentionally didn’t sketch out the details of how Chrom became a lancer or what he was doing for whom. I’m aware this is riding the edge of what Lancer says about cloning, but I figure “Someone tries something on the edge of what’s possible, it works for a while, and then goes badly” is a perfectly fair plot. I do know that the main outward sign of Cooper’s abilities (improved reactions, etc) is her hair, which is mineral-green, grows very slowly, and has the toughness and some of the feel of fiber-optic cable. Her skin can also have a faint green patina in some lights.

pc's/backstory/evelyn-garve-(backstory)..title: Evelyn Garve (Backstory)
pc's/backstory/evelyn-garve-(backstory)..description: Evelyn Garve is about 5'8 and fairly lean muscle. She doesn't give off the aura of a lancer, and has burn scars on both her arm and parts of her face. She wears dark sunglasses most of the time. Her eyes are HORUS tech that sometimes glitch out on her and are difficult for others to look at too long.

pc's/backstory/veda-sulati-(backstory).h.title: Veda Sulati (Backstory)
pc's/backstory/veda-sulati-(backstory).h.description:
The last set of four hangar bays had red warning lights flashing and a passive message of 'potentially hostile' scrolling across the screen. It was the sort of thing usually reserved for malfunctioning mechs that might unload a few rounds haphazardly due to pressure or some fried wiring. It was also what they usually did for one pilot whose reputation preceded her. Veda Sulati was young - not uncommon for pilots especially out here - but the Sulati name was well-known for the late patriarch's achievements in extra-military skirmishes and bounties. With the stakes he played at, it was one failed job that ended him permanently.
Veda had either inherited or grown into her father's boisterous, self-confident approach to piloting. What she had inherited was his NHP, which she affectionately designated Raiju. Unlike the more common Sisyphus systems, something must have happened either with her tinkering or the late Sulati's last stand. Raiju was not a system you just shut down; in fact, the NHP had taken over the whole of Veda's Pegasus chassis like some amalgamated puppet. Or, at least, that's the rumors. Back Veda into a corner and Raiju would come for you, the Pegasus moving and arming without pilot intervention or action. But those were just rumors, right? Plenty of weird things happened in the dark space between a pilot, their mech, and the NHP networks that behaved like a neural net interface. Plenty of things far weirder than rumors of yet another mech that moved and acted unbidden.(Veda's callsign is Tora. Her father's was one of three: Tora, Tiger, or Raijin)(Veda's dad died likely when she was about 10. She's been piloting since then. Nothing officially on the books until she was 12 or 13 tho)Beyond the hazard lights, the faint sounds of a drumline bounced between the metal walls. It wasn't rock and roll, and it wasn't exactly classical, though it certainly felt more towards the traditional side. A few flashes of light and stray sparks down the bays marked real activity. The bay surrounding the Sulati Pegasus looked less like a parking space and more like the storage unit the crazy uncle would rent to escape his roommates. There wasn't exactly a bed, but the pile of clothes and blankets were pretty indicative of intent. From one hook in the wall a piece of fabric hung down like a canopy, providing a dark spot from the overhead lights. There was even an electric kettle set next to a crockpot and a coffeepot, all over-plugged into an extension cord running into the Pegasus. Tools, all well-used and relatively well-kept but showing their age, were scattered around the area. It wasn't exactly unnerving to see the cockpit and 'head' region of the Pegasus turn towards your approach, though some people experienced a sinking of their stomachs if they'd then saw Veda with a laptop propped in one arm while the other typed standing beside the moving mech. The nineteen year old was dressed in brown, grease-stained overalls, one strap hanging loose with a series of wire cutters and tools peeking out of the various pockets. She did look up when a slight crackling noise somewhere between static and a person tsking boomed from a speaker that had to be tucked behind the Pegasus armor. The sound was chased by a mechanized voice, "Approaching." Veda looked up, surprised to see someone other than one of the techs or stationmasters approaching. "Y' lost?""Nah." The teenage boy walking down the hall grinned, pointing up into the bay to his immediate right. "Just a neighbor!" His careworn military jumpsuit clashed slightly with his carefree demeanor; judging from his looks he probably wasn't behind her by more than a year, but he seemed younger anyway. He unslung his backpack taking care to put the omnihook computer terminal he'd been carrying gently against it, along one of the bay's large bulkheads. The bay he pointed to held another Horus frame, a Hydra-pattern whose four arms rested against its body, attached by clamps that wouldn't stand up to close scrutiny or conventional physics. Slithering sounds and odd bulges sometimes came from inside its hunched back, a mark of the pseudosentience that controlled its drones. If the Pegasus had a conscious mind of its own, then Hydra was dreaming. "I heard they put us down here and had to take a look." He noticed the Pegasus eyeing him, and took a single step back before giving it a friendly wave. "Heey, that's awesome. So, you're really Veda Sulati, huh? Name's Sully, nice to meet you."Veda peered into the bay he'd gestured, frowning lightly. "Must've come in during off hours. Hydra, with drones? You know they originally thought the drones would be used strictly for surveillance - suits always get shit wrong." "-'Nice' is superfluous, state of perceived existence. We have now met.-" The Pegasus didn't move farther than it had already, though it pulled a laugh from Veda. "Always to the point, Raiju, fair enough. Yeah, I'm Veda. I do think it's nice to see someone not pissing themselves over tech that shouldn't surprise them." She took a few steps towards Sully, extending her hand. "You're stuck with us on the next job, from the bay assignment?""I didn't know that! Although there are a ton of different ones-- mine's not that tricked out, and there's half a dozen kinds in there. Can see why they'd be tricky to work out." Sully laughed along with Veda as he shook her hand. "Yeah, he's got me there. But I figure, either you guys don't make a habit out of rampaging around mech bays, or somebody doesn't like me very much. And whichever one it is, I'm probably better off on your side." "Yeah, looks that way, if you got on contract yesterday. It sounds like a lot more fun, now that I know I'll get to see you in action.""Drones just feel like a lot to me. I'm impressed with anyone that can handle them, NHP-aided or not." She reached out the hand not balancing her laptop, and the Pegasus dipped its 'head' down to touch it gently. "Cut the taiko down, alright? Don't want to yell over the music." "-Acquiesce.-" The music - a type of ancient drum pattern from the early days of Earth - faded into the background. "We're alright on mission. Raiju's rep is just off-the-field. Amazing the number of pilots who are happy to push around a kid and get real nervous when the heat shows up. I was always taught never to take a knife to a gun fight, and I like my guns big and incomprehensible." Veda shrugged lightly. "Sounds like we're in the same gig. And it sounds like an easy one with a desperate client." "-Greater than 82.3% chance of higher than average payout. Stipend terms are credit only. Salvage during mission best course of action for new parts." Veda rolled her eyes. "Yeah, yeah, yeah, I know, you corroded a feeder line when the last gun decided to warp in an acid bath. I already promised it'll get fixed after payout. It's a non-critical component, and you know it. You just want a new toy."

pc's/mechs/dream.html.title: Dream
pc's/mechs/dream.html.description:
Hunter 1/round, the Mourning Cloak may deal +1d6 bonus damage on hit with a melee attack if its target has no adjacent characters, or if the Mourning Cloak is the only character adjacent to the target. Biotic Components The Mourning Cloak gains +1 accuracy on Agility checks and saves. Blinkspace Jump Teleport to a space within range 3d6. You don’t require line of sight, but attempts to teleport to occupied spaces cause you to remain stationary and lose this action. If you roll the same number on all three dice, you disappear until your group rests, at which point you reappear nearby. Stabilize Singularity For the rest of the scene, you teleport when you Boost or make a standard move. Overpower Caliber
Variable Sword
Variable Sword / Fold Knife // Overpower Caliber
Fade Cloak, Hunter Logic Suite, Singularity Motivator, Type-3 Projected Shield

pc's/mechs/maya.html.title: Maya
pc's/mechs/maya.html.description:
Metastatic Paralysis
When an attack roll against the Gorgon lands on 1–2, it automatically misses and the attacker becomes Stunned until the end of their next turn.
Gaze
The Gorgon can take two reactions per turn, instead of one.
Guardian
Adjacent allied characters may use the Gorgon for hard cover.

pc's/mechs/prototype.html.title: Prototype
pc's/mechs/prototype.html.description:
Grapple Cable
The Blackbeard can Grapple targets within Range 5. If the Grapple is successful, the Blackbeard is immediately pulled adjacent to the target by the most direct path. If there are no suitable spaces, the grapple breaks and the Blackbeard does not move.
Lock/Kill Subsystem
While grappling, the Blackbeard can Boost and take reactions.
Exposed Reactor
The Blackbeard receives +1 Difficulty on ENGINEERING checks and saves.
Quick action
This system fires grappling harpoons at any number of targets within Range 5 and line of sight. Affected characters must succeed on a Hull save or take 2d6 Kinetic damage and be knocked Prone, then pulled adjacent to you, or as close as possible. They become Immobilized until the end of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are otherwise unaffected.

pc's/mechs/raiju-(mech).html.title: Raiju (Mech)
pc's/mechs/raiju-(mech).html.description: ¿%:?EXTR!UDE GUN GUN: GUN By the way, I know everything When it would roll damage, the Pegasus can instead deal the average damage based on the number of dice rolled, as follows: 1d3 (2), 1d6 (4), 2d6 (7), 3d6 (11), 4d6 (14). This must be decided before rolling damage. [ERROR] [range 15][1 AP Kinetic Damage] Your mech’s omnigun is a piece of experimental hardware so advanced that it defies physics: it doesn’t require a mount, nor does it have a weapon type or size – meaning that it can’t be modified or benefit from your talents. You can’t attack normally with this weapon. Instead, 1/round, as a free action during your turn, you can use it to deal 1 AP kinetic damage to a character within Range 15 and line of sight. This doesn’t count as an attack, hits automatically, ignores cover, bypasses Immunity, and its damage can’t be reduced or ignored in any way. No rule in this book or any other supersedes this. Fire Omnigun 1/round, as a free action during your turn, deal 1 AP kinetic damage to a character within Range 15 and line of sight. This doesn’t count as an attack, hits automatically, ignores cover, bypasses Immunity, and its damage can’t be reduced or ignored in any way. No rule supersedes this. For the rest of this scene, you can use the Ushabti Omnigun 3/round, instead of 1/round. The Lesson of the Held Image

references/core-bonus/overpower-caliber..title: Overpower Caliber
references/core-bonus/overpower-caliber..description: Choose one weapon. 1/round, when you hit with an attack, you can cause it to deal +1d6 bonus damage.

references/notes/2025-12-14-government-s.title: 2025-12-14 Government Sponsored Eldritch Horrors
references/notes/2025-12-14-government-s.description:
We have arrived at a large spaceport; the capital city of Steralis.Steralis is fairly remote. It's a border Union planet, far enough out they have their own monarchy and as long as they don't make trouble, no one bothers them about it. Things at the province level are fairly autonomous. After the death of the previous king, there have been some power struggles and no regency has been defined yet. We've been summoned because there's some kind of group rebeling against the monarchy, and the group seems to be much better resourced than expected. Trying to research the struggles from off-planet is a little difficult because you can find records of incidents but no explanations of what triggered them. Ethan: "There is no one at the level of Baron Puppy Kicker."
Cooper Ellys and Veda Sulati arrive and sign up together, the initial contract is a little vague as they're not going to assign people to lancer units until they know how many people they have. As lancers we get small private rooms like a hotel. While waiting, Cooper wanders her hallway and finds someone (Giani) to play cards with in an attempt to gather information. Ethan: "What kind of, are you going for like a child's card game or poker?" Angela: "She brought her yu-gi-oh deck." Cooper suggests a simple game of poker and Giani suggests making it more interesting with some very strong drinks. Cooper agrees to have one over the course of the game, and Giani says he'll just drink her share, which makes the game get much easier as they go. Cooper idly asks if he's heard of any names from her list, but he hasn't.
Evelyn Garve takes longer to read the contract out of concern it will lock her out of her usual work. She thinks it looks odd but her NHP assures her it's fine. Chris: "You just run it through your NHP and it says 'become ungovernable.'" Angela rolls again to see if she believes the NHP. "That's a one!" Chris: "It took you two rolls."
Eventually we receive a summons to meet in room and report to Officer Lance Beal. Chris: "Really? Lance Lancer?" Ethan: "Yes." Angela: "Lance Lancer." Jessee: "Does he have a dragonite?" Ethan: "He does not." Angela: "Corporal Lance Lancer." Cooper and Veda get there first and catch up briefly, Evelyn arrives and introduces herself. Evelyn recognizes Veda's name, and we do brief introductions. Angela: "It'd be weirder if I didn't know your name, right?" Chris: "You made your DC 10 bardic knowledge roll." Angela: "Did I?" Chris: "You didn't roll it, so yes."
We discuss what everyone pilots and that if there are three of us in a unit they must be dealing with something formidable. After a bit Lance enters and greets us. He tells us this is his first command and we've been stationed at Tal Astero.They've been getting reports from rural residents about being harassed by insurgents with mechs, some of which have been odd. The royal garrison has been unable to find anything or stop it. The locals have petitioned for support, and we're being sent. It's been hard for them to get a count of how many insurgents there are. They've confirmed 5 unique units, but since they're not sure how mobile the insurgents are they estimate it could be up to 50. Lance clarifies the insurgents are attacking smaller settlements to steal resources, such as ores. They don't have any footage.Veda is concerned at the lack of information we have to work with, but there's not much else to be done for it. Evelyn and Cooper agree we can go talk to the locals and see what we can find. We confirm there's no intelligence support out there, but supplies us with a map and markings for where the attacks have happened. Lance says he understands some of this operation may be just waiting for them to strike and moving when they do, hopefully quickly enough to catch them. He'd rather us attempt to capture and interrogate someone.We discuss and determine that we should have a shot caller in the field. Cooper points out she'll blink out of reality in her mech so she might not be an optimal choice, and since Veda has more combat experience than Evelyn, we nominate Veda.After plenty of back-and-forth, he tells us he'll take us to the good cafeteria, which we are amused by. Chris: "No one talks about the secret cafeteria. We're Lancers, we're literally fight club."
Lance takes us to the officer cafeteria, where Artria and Gwen wave us over. Lance introduces us all, Artria assures us she's already secured the best food and tells us to dig in. We talk about what we all pilot, and confirm that we're all working the same problem but won't be getting deployed to the same location. Ethan: "Gwen mech's is called Spiral Dragon." Angela: "Spiral Dragon! Sorry, it's an exciting name." Ethan: "If you press, she says she inherited the mech when she was like eight and named it, then decided not to change it because she thought it was cool when she was eight and it turns out she was right." Chris: "Yeah, I'd name a mech Spiral Dragon now." Angela: "I respect the hell out of that. 'I named it that when I was eight, and it turns out, that still fucks'." It turns out all of them are native to Steralis and to Tal Astero and have known each other since childhood. Angela: "Oh no, we're the side characters in their anime." We gather that Tal Astero is a province being ran by Governor Artem Pless, who is genuinely doing his best but is being overwhelmed by the insurgent situation. He's gone to the imperial garrison a few times for help but has been getting nothing. Cooper tries to get more specifics about what Pless needs help with.Evelyn brings up that there were rumors of HORUS tech mixed up in this insurgency, and we talk about those rumors but no one has anything concrete. As we're discussing it, Evelyn's eye glitch out; she briefly takes off her sunglasses and explains they're artificial and act up sometimes.
Veda and Cooper feel someone pat them on the back. Veda instinctively panics and punches them, Cooper leans out of the way, and Evelyn jumps up. The person apologizes and says they deserved that, and asks if they're the pilots of Dream and |Raiju. She'd seen the names arriving and wanted to meet with them. Veda is still standing and Cooper tells her to sit down and introduce herself; she's Regional Commander Sonja Creen. She's familiar with Raiju from reputation but wants to know how Cooper came to acquire Dream. She says she knows the previous owner and he was an asshole, which Cooper agrees with. They agree they'd like to talk at more length about Dream.Veda us still on her feet and unclearly ill at ease. Commander tells her that she knew of her father and she's happy to see someone carrying on that tradition. She heads off to the infirmary, since her nose is still bleeding. Cooper: "How do you think it's going?" Commander: "It's a pain in the ass." Veda: "The food?" Commander: "This whole effort." Angela: "If it's the food, you might want to see a doctor about that." Ethan: "The pepper was too much spice for her." Chris: "This, our regional commander." Angela: "She just comes up to people and is like 'this food'." Jessee: " 'I'm just shitting fire'. " Chris: "We thought she was invading our personal space, but her ass was on afterburner. She was just trying to stop." Ethan: "The game literally has heat mechanics, so." Chris: "She was tech attacked by that chili." The rest of the table was somewhat stunned into silence, but conversation continues. Evelyn compliments Veda on the punch, and we all sit back down. Veda is clearly uneasy but we eventually all settle back down, and end up talking about the differences in food between planet and space, and then mechs. Ethan: "HORUS would make a mind goblin." Angela chokes on food. Jessee: "Mind goblin?" Angela: "Mind gobblin' these nuts?" Evelyn asks again about the rumored HORUS tech, and the local share they've got some scraps of equipment, that they think is HORUS, but it's been difficult to confirm much of anything. There are no Lancers on planet with HORUS licenses who have been willing to share. Angela, at the prospect of being in charge of shot-calling: "I cast gun, targeting myself." Veda takes Evelyn to meet Raiju. On the way, Evelyn explains more about her eyes being HORUS tech. Veda introduces Evelyn to Raiju, and nothing goes wrong.
Cooper goes to talk to the Regional Commander about Dream, and explains that the previous pilot, Chrom Ellys was her father and she inherited the licenses from him. The commander explains she and Chrom had done business in the past; she found him professional but knows he was not exactly on the up and up. Cooper explains she knows not everyone who had business with him thinks well of him, so she's trying to proactively seek out anyone in that boat. Commander assures her that she isn't looking for any kind of vengeance, and that if she knows any of the names Cooper is looking for she'd be happy to help. The commander says she doesn't know anyone on-planet who had particularly strong feelings about him, but that he was also very tight-lipped about his business. Cooper suspects not even her father knew everyone who cared about his business, but is happy to know there are no obvious threats.Cooper asks for her impression of Lance; she hasn't seen a ton out of him yet, but thinks he has promise. She's concerned he has a lot to do to find his own voice, but she's not worried.The next morning, we're given directions on where we'll get deployed to, which is near some mountains. Our mechs will be delivered via train, so we get them all loaded up and begin our journey.
Veda asks Cooper how her conversation with the commander went, and she catches her up. Evelyn asks how the two of them knew each other and Cooper explains they met at Kepler's Pride, their fathers had been involved in some parallel work, and they'd stayed in touch. Chris: "So have we finished our train ride yet?" Ethan: "Yes." Jessee: "Veda is unimpressed by the mountains in the distance." Angela: "I'm the normal amount of impressed. Whatever that value should be." Ethan: "Seven." Jessee: "No scale, just seven." Chris: "You can't say he's wrong." We make it to a small factory town, where we unlock our mechs. Ethan: "The locals are trying to determine the OSHA guidelines for touching the floating back orb on Raiju."
Someone approaches and introduces himself as Heath Ledersin; confirms we're here to help, and tells us to let him know what they can help with. We get as settled in as we plan to; they've built us a make-shift mech hanger. Lancer and the other lancers are continuing on to the other side of the settlements, so we bid them bye for now.Once we've settled in, Cooper goes out and talks to the locals to try to get any footage the locals might have about what's been attacking them. She finds someone who has a low quality recording of a small mining outpost; it has two or three mechs bursting out of the tree lines, kill everything on their way to the ore deposits, then take what they can carry and leave. They're not lancer frames, and seem to be poorly assembled collections of parts.She brings it back to Evelyn and Veda; Evelyn looks at it and after a moment is able to identify specific parts of the mechs that are clearly HORUS. As far as we can tell it looks like the mechs shouldn't be functioning but the HORUS tech seems to be helping somehow. Cooper asks Evelyn how she could tell, and Evelyn explains her eyes, and that she came to Steralis because she kept seeing the name appear places she knew it wasn't printed. Veda goes and hunts down some supplies, finding us some extra repairs.We meet back up with Heath, who gives us a more detailed sitrep. Mostly the small mining towns have been getting attacked, the attackers have left the factory alone. They're brutally killing whoever is there, but have been weirdly efficient about it. Sometimes tech goes haywire around the same times, and some people claim they've seen things in the mountain that aren't there, or routes appear different, including being walled off. Cooper asks if they think it's related to HORUS tech, he believes yes but they have no proof. He says the attacks have been recently ramping up, they're attacking a few times a week and but it's been a couple days since they were spotted, so we're about due.We determine best we can do for now is set up some patrols and wait until they strike. We exchange communications information. Chris: "I am not going off in my futuristic battle mech to go confirm Reddit rumors. I have few principles, but that's one." Angela: "If anyone posts about Yu-gi-oh, though, you're on it." We get a good idea of the lay of the land, but it's an uneventful day, as are the next two.On the third day, we get an alert that a town 20 minutes from us has been attacked. We immediately head off and arrive to find the ore depot ransacked and some dead bodies, with one living person who is alarmed to see us. He points us in the direction the mechs went, and we track them.We are fighting a Rainmaker, a Witch, and a Ronin, who we find hauling the ore.Veda unleashes on the Rainmaker and takes it out with the omnigun and one autogun shot. She directs the second autogun at the Ronin, which deflects the bullet. Ethan: "This is a good time for the witch to go." Jessee: "There's never a good time for the witch to go." Chris: "That's so true."
The Witch blinds Veda, which spreads to Maya. It attempts to attack Veda again but misses, and it is jammed by Maya's Scorpion. Angela: "You may have blinded me, but get fucked." Ethan: "And she is going to go off and get fucked." Angela: "May I cordially invite you to get fucked." Evelyn deploys her sensor Sentinel Drone.The Ronin moves in, taking damage from the drone, and attacks Evelyn. Ethan: "And he is going to..." Angela: "I thought you were about to say he died." Ethan: "No, the opposite. He crit you." Jessee: "For Angela, that's about the same thing." Cooper moves in and strikes the Ronin, then disengages and moves to the Witch. Chris: "I am going to approach the Ronin, and challenge it to a weeb-off." Cooper attacks the Witch and destroys it.The Ronin moves away from the sentinel drone. Evelyn misses her attack of opportunity and it crits her again, doing structure damage. Ethan: "Are we playing with the alternate structure damage rules?" Chris: "I would like to." Ethan: "So don't mark it on your sheet, with the current rules if you roll badly, your mech just explodes." Angela: "I did just roll a one, so." Chris: "Yeah, Angela is in this campaign. We can't use those rules." Evelyn repositions her drone, takes a small hit from the Ronin, and then knocks it prone.Veda shoots the Ronin; it redirects one shot back to her, but nearly kills it.Veda moves away from the Ronin and shoots with the omnigun, killing it.

headers.heading: Meeting Lance and getting our orders

references/places/eschaton.html.title: Eschaton
references/places/eschaton.html.description: Cooper Ellys's home planet.

references/places/steralis.html.title: Steralis
references/places/steralis.html.description: Steralis is fairly remote. It's a border Union planet, far enough out they have their own monarchy and as long as they don't make trouble, no one bothers them about it. Things at the province level are fairly autonomous. After the death of the previous king, Sten Stera, there have been some power struggles and no regency has been defined yet. Hans Stera is the current child-king.

references/places/tal-astero.html.title: Tal Astero
references/places/tal-astero.html.description: A province of Steralis. Governed by Governer Artem Pless.

references/systems/fade-cloak.html.title: Fade Cloak
references/systems/fade-cloak.html.description:
Activate Fade Cloak When activated, you immediately move out of phase with realspace, becoming intangible. While intangible, you may move through obstructions, but not end your turn within them. You cannot interact with any other object or character or be interacted with in any way (e.g., taking or dealing damage).
Roll 1d6 at the start of each of your turns: on 3 or less, you return to realspace until the start of your next turn; on 4+, you remain intangible.
This system remains active for the rest of the scene, or until you deactivate it as a quick action.

references/systems/hunter-logic-suite.ht.title: Hunter Logic Suite
references/systems/hunter-logic-suite.ht.description: Stalk Prey You infect the target with a viral logic that wipes your image from their sensors. They treat you as Invisible until you next take damage from them. This can only affect one target at a time. Terrify You infect the target with a viral logic that makes your mech appear horrifying. Until the end of their next turn, they become Impaired and cannot make any voluntary movements that bring them closer to you.

references/systems/singularity-motivator.title: Singularity Motivator
references/systems/singularity-motivator.description: Exposed Singularity 1/Round Your mech takes damage You may immediately teleport to a free space within 1d6 spaces

references/systems/type-3-projected-shie.title: Type-3 Projected Shield
references/systems/type-3-projected-shie.description: Project Shield Nominate a character within line of sight: all ranged or melee attacks that they make against you or that you make against them gain +2 Difficulty until the start of your next turn.

references/talents/brawler.html.title: Brawler
references/talents/brawler.html.description:
You gain +1 Accuracy on all melee attacks against targets you are Grappling.our Improvised Attacks gain Knockback 2 and deal 2d6+2 Kinetic damage.Gain a Brawler Die, 1d6 starting at 6. Each time you Grapple, Ram, or make an Improvised Attack, lower the value of the Brawler Die by 1. When the Brawler Die reaches 1, you may reset it to 6 and, as a full action, make a Knockout Blow against an adjacent character. The value of your Brawler Die persists between scenes, but it resets to 6 when you rest or perform a Full Repair.Knockout Blow Reset your Brawler Die to 6 and, as a full action, make a Knockout Blow against an adjacent character. They must pass a Hull save, or take 2d6+2 Kinetic damage and become Stunned until the end of their next turn.
This action can only be taken when the value of your Brawler Die is 1

references/talents/combined-arms.html.title: Combined Arms
references/talents/combined-arms.html.description: As long as you’re Engaged, you and any allies adjacent to you count as having soft cover.You don’t gain Difficulty from being Engaged.Whenever you hit a character with a melee attack, you gain +1 Accuracy on your next ranged attack against them; and, whenever you hit a character with a ranged attack, you gain +1 Accuracy on your next melee attack against them. This effect doesn’t stack.

references/talents/duelist.html.title: Duelist
references/talents/duelist.html.description:
Gain +1 Accuracy on the first melee attack you make with a Main Melee weapon on your turn.1/round, when you hit with a Main Melee weapon, you gain 1 Blademaster Die – a d6 – up to a maximum of 3 Blademaster Die. They last until expended or the current scene ends. You can expend Blademaster Dice 1-for-1 for the following:
Parry: As a reaction when you’re hit by a melee attack, you gain Resistance to all damage, heat, and burn dealt by the attack.
Deflect: As a reaction when you’re hit by a ranged attack, you may roll any number of Blademaster Dice, expending them: if you roll a 5+ on any of these dice, you gain Resistance to all damage, heat, and burn dealt by the attack.
Feint: As a free action, choose an adjacent character: when moving, you ignore engagement and don’t provoke reactions from your target until the start of your next turn.
Trip: As a quick action, choose an adjacent character: they must pass an Agility save or fall Prone. Whatever the result, you may freely pass through their space until the end of your current turn, although you can’t end your turn in their space.
1/round, when you hit with a melee attack on your turn, you may spend a Blademaster Die to immediately Grapple or Ram your target as a free action after the attack has been resolved.

references/talents/executioner.html.title: Executioner
references/talents/executioner.html.description: 1/round, when you hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you can make another melee attack with the same weapon as a free action against a different character within Threat and line of sight. This attack deals half damage, if successful.The first time in a round that you perform a critical hit with a Heavy or Superheavy melee weapon, you deal 3 Kinetic damage to all characters and objects of your choice within Threat, other than the one you just attacked.1/round, when you miss with a melee attack, you reroll it against a different target within Threat and line of sight.

references/talents/hunter.html.title: Hunter
references/talents/hunter.html.description: 1/round, when you attack with an Auxiliary melee weapon, you may fly up to 3 spaces directly towards the targeted character before the attack. This movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.All your Auxiliary melee weapons gain Thrown 5, if they don’t have this property already – if they already have Thrown, it increases to Thrown 5. At the end of your turn, all weapons you have thrown this turn automatically return to you.1/round, when you hit a character with a melee attack, you may also throw an Auxiliary melee weapon as an attack against any character within Range as a free action. This attack can’t deal bonus damage.

references/talents/leader.html.title: Leader
references/talents/leader.html.description:
Gain 3 Leadership Dice, which are d6s, and gain the Issue Order Free Action.
You can’t use Leadership Dice from other characters as long as you have any remaining. If you have none, you regain 1 Leadership Die when you rest or regain all when you perform a Full Repair.
Leadership dice are consumed when expended. Issue Order 1/turn, on your turn as a free action, you may issue an order to an allied PC you can communicate with, describing a course of action, and give them a Leadership Die. They may either expend the Leadership Die to gain +1 Accuracy on any action that directly follows from that order, or they may return it to you as a free action. Allies can have one Leadership Die at a time, which lasts until used or until the end of the current scene.
Gain 5 Leadership Dice instead of 3; additionally, you can now issue a command as a reaction at the start of any other player’s turn, any number of times per round. Issue Command Another player starts their turn. You may issue a command to an allied PC you can communicate with, describing a course of action, and give them a Leadership Die. They may either expend the Leadership Die to gain +1 Accuracy on any action that directly follows from that order, or they may return it to you as a free action. Allies can have one Leadership Die at a time, which lasts until used or until the end of the current scene.
Gain 6 Leadership Dice instead of 5. Allies that have your Leadership Dice can expend them to reduce damage by –1d6 when taking damage or to deal +1d6 bonus damage when they hit with an attack.

references/talents/siege-specialist.html.title: Siege Specialist
references/talents/siege-specialist.html.description:
Following action 1/roundQuick action
Fire a jackhammer round from an underslung launcher, automatically dealing 10 AP Kinetic damage to a Size 1 section of any object within Range (e.g., cover, deployable equipment, buildings, or terrain). Any characters adjacent to your target are knocked back from it by 2 spaces and take 2 Kinetic damage.
1/round, before rolling an attack with a Cannon, all characters adjacent to you must succeed on a Hull save or be knocked back by 1 space and knocked Prone. You are then pushed 1 space in any direction.1/round, when you perform a critical hit on a character or object with a Cannon, you may choose to cause an explosion of secondary munitions, causing a Burst 2 explosion around your target. Characters within the affected area must either drop Prone as a reaction, or take 2 Explosive damage and be knocked back by 2 spaces from the center of the attack.

references/talents/skirmisher.html.title: Skirmisher
references/talents/skirmisher.html.description: At the start of your turn, you gain soft cover. You lose this cover if you attack or force another character to make a save.Before or after you Skirmish (including as a reaction, e.g. from Overwatch), you may move 2 spaces. This movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.The first attack taken as a reaction against you in any round automatically misses.

references/core-bonus/the-lesson-of-the-.title: The Lesson of the Held Image
references/core-bonus/the-lesson-of-the-.description: Reaction 1/round Any allied character starts their turn Make a Lock On tech action against any character within line of sight and Sensors.
references/core-bonus/the-lesson-of-the-.sourcePath: References/Core Bonus/The Lesson of the Held Image.md

references/talents/crack-shot.html.title: Crack Shot
references/talents/crack-shot.html.description:
Protocol
Become Immobilized until the start of your next turn but gain +1 Accuracy on all attacks you make with Rifles.

references/talents/heavy-gunner.html.title: Heavy Gunner
references/talents/heavy-gunner.html.description:
Quick action
Choose a character within line of sight and Range of one of your Heavy ranged weapons, and within 10 spaces: they are Impaired until the start of your next turn. For the duration, if your target moves more than 1 space, they clear Impaired, but you may attack them as a reaction with a Heavy ranged weapon for half damage, Heat, or Burn, and then this effect ends. You can make this attack at any point during their movement (e.g., waiting until they exit cover).
Covering Fire can only affect one character at a time – subsequent uses replace previous ones – and it immediately ends if your target damages you. Reaction 1/Round The target of your Covering Fire moves more than 1 space. You may attack the target with a Heavy ranged weapon for half damage, Heat, or Burn. You can make this attack at any point during their movement (e.g., waiting until they exit cover).

references/talents/stormbringer.html.title: Stormbringer
references/talents/stormbringer.html.description: 1/round, when you successfully attack with a Launcher and consume Lock On, you may also knock your target Prone.

references/talents/technophile.html.title: Technophile
references/talents/technophile.html.description:
You have developed a custom NHP. This NHP can speak to you and has a personality, but they are less advanced than most NHPs and are incapable of independent thought, relying on you for direction. When acting alone, they will follow the last direction given and defend themself as needed; however, they have limited initiative and don’t benefit from your talents.
You may choose for your mech to gain the Servant-Class NHP System

inbox/archived/call-to-arms!.html.title: Call To Arms!
inbox/archived/call-to-arms!.html.description:
From: Henry Decker Subject: Open Contract
King Sten Stera of Steralis has passed away unexpectedly at the age of 28. His heir Hans Stera is only 6 years old, so advisors and nobles have formed a council to take care of the kingdom until he is of age to take over. However, in the chaos following the unexpected death several insurgent groups have arisen. Normally they would be handled by the local police and militia, however they appear to have acquired a cache of HORUS tech and are proving elusive. In this time of turmoil, we humbly request the aid of any willing to take up arms to restore peace in these troubled times.

inbox/congratulations-and-registration.h.title: Congratulations and Registration
inbox/congratulations-and-registration.h.description:
Sender: The office of Henry Decker Subject: Congratulations and Registration
Attachments: (Company Name) RosterThank you for heeding the call for assistance. We appreciate your rapid response, and apologize for throwing you into the field so soon after you arrived. After reviewing your combat logs, we are most grateful to have you on board. We would also like to add that you fought surprisingly well as a unit. You are not obligated to, but if you would like to register as a company it would make it easier to ensure you all end up on the same contracts in the future. I've attached the form for your convenience.

inbox/mission-follow-up.html.title: Mission Follow-up
inbox/mission-follow-up.html.description: Sender: Lance Beal Subject: Follow-up on the suppliesHello! First of all, I would like to thank you all for your assistance on the last mission, we could not have cleared that outpost without you. Unfortunately, it seems that the supplies confiscated by the imperial garrison have not made it back to the local towns yet. I hate to ask for your assistance again, but if you could help me remind them where the supplies need to go it might jog their memories. And bring your mech, they may need help carrying all that heavy cargo.

references/core-bonus/the-lesson-of-the-.sourcePath: References/Core Bonus/The Lesson of the Held Image.md

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site

bodyClasses: publish css-settings-manager styled-scrollbars show-inline-title show-ribbon is-focused
